params.name: message_id
params.description: The ID of the message to fetch delivery logs for.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of delivery logs for the specified message.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

params.name: tenant
params.description: Limits the results to items with the corresponding tenant.

params.name: channel_id
params.description: Limits the results to items with the corresponding channel ID.

params.name: status[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages with the given delivery status.

params.name: engagement_status[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages with the given engagement status.

params.name: message_ids[]
params.description: Limits the results to only the message IDs given (max 50). Note: when using this option, the results will be subject to any other filters applied to the query.

params.name: workflow_categories[]
params.description: Limits the results to messages related to any of the provided categories.

params.name: source
params.description: Limits the results to messages triggered by the given workflow key.

params.name: workflow_run_id
params.description: Limits the results to messages associated with the top-level workflow run ID returned by the workflow trigger request.

params.name: workflow_recipient_run_id
params.description: Limits the results to messages for a specific recipient's workflow run.

params.name: trigger_data
params.description: Limits the results to only messages that were generated with the given data. See [trigger data filtering](/api-reference/overview/trigger-data-filtering) for more information.

params.name: inserted_at.gt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.gte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted after or on the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lt
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before the given date.

params.name: inserted_at.lte
params.description: Limits the results to items inserted before or on the given date.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of messages for the current environment.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `unseen`. This reverses the `seen` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).
Marks the given messages as unarchived. This reverses the `archived` state. Archived messages are hidden from the default message list in the feed but can still be accessed and unarchived later.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `unread`. This reverses the `read` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `seen`. This indicates that the user has viewed the message in their feed or inbox. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `unseen`. This reverses the `seen` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The ID of the message to fetch events for.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of events for the specified message.

Marks the given messages as `seen`. This indicates that the user has viewed the message in their feed or inbox. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).
Marks the given messages as `unseen`. This reverses the `seen` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `read`. This indicates that the user has read the message content. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `unread`. This reverses the `read` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_ids[]
params.description: The IDs of the messages to fetch contents of.

items.docs: Get the contents of multiple messages in a single request.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The ID of the message to fetch contents of.

items.docs: Returns the fully rendered contents of a message, where the response depends on which channel the message was sent through.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The ID of the message to fetch activities for.

params.name: trigger_data
params.description: The trigger data to filter activities by.

params.name: after
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries after.

params.name: before
params.description: The cursor to fetch entries before.

params.name: page_size
params.description: The number of items per page (defaults to 50).

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of activities for the specified message.
Marks the given messages as `unread`. This reverses the `read` state. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Archives a message for the user. Archived messages are hidden from the default message list in the feed but can still be accessed and unarchived later.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Removes a message from the archived state, making it visible in the default message list in the feed again.
Marks the given messages as archived. Archived messages are hidden from the default message list in the feed but can still be accessed and unarchived later.

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Marks a message as `interacted` with by the user. This can include any user action on the message, with optional metadata about the specific interaction. Cannot include more than 5 key-value pairs, must not contain nested data. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Removes a message from the archived state, making it visible in the default message list in the feed again.
Marks the given messages as `read`. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).

params.name: message_id
params.description: The unique identifier for the message.

items.docs: Retrieves a specific message by its ID.

Marks the given messages as interacted with by the user. This can include any user action on the message, with optional metadata about the specific interaction. Cannot include more than 5 key-value pairs, must not contain nested data. Read more about message engagement statuses [here](/send-notifications/message-statuses#engagement-status).
